pi_items.source: tshark analysis of smtp.pcap file via 'tshark -r smtp.pcap | wc -l'
tshark analysis of smtp.pcap file via 'tshark -r smtp.pcap -Y "smtp" | wc -l'
tshark extraction of MAIL FROM parameter: 'tshark -Y "smtp.req.command == MAIL" -T fields -e smtp.req.parameter' normalized to lowercase and stripped of brackets
tshark extraction of RCPT TO parameter: 'tshark -Y "smtp.req.command == RCPT" -T fields -e smtp.req.parameter' normalized to lowercase and stripped of brackets
tshark extraction of source IP from 220 SMTP greeting response: 'tshark -Y "smtp.response.code == 220" -T fields -e ip.src'
tshark extraction of destination IP from 220 SMTP greeting response: 'tshark -Y "smtp.response.code == 220" -T fields -e ip.dst'
install_wireshark.sh script lines 100-103 download_pcap function calls

privileged_info_summary: This is an SMTP email forensics task using the official Wireshark sample capture file smtp.pcap (real, non-synthetic data). The PCAP file contains 60 total packets with 32 SMTP protocol packets. The task requires extraction of email headers from the captured SMTP session: sender gurpartap@patriots.in, recipient raj_deol2002in@yahoo.co.in, SMTP server at IP 74.53.140.153, and SMTP client at IP 10.10.1.4. All data comes from Wireshark's publicly hosted sample captures via wiki.wireshark.org.
